_meta.keyKnowledge: We learn when and how to suspend judgement to support generating and evaluating ideas and possibilities.

_source.w: We learn when and how to suspend judgement to support generating and evaluating ideas and possibilities.
y: Suspending judgement allows us to consider possibilities that premature evaluation would eliminate.
This discipline is essential for creative thinking, fair evaluation and open-minded inquiry.
Knowing when to hold back judgement — and when to apply it — is a sophisticated thinking skill.
eg: We practise withholding judgement during brainstorming to allow more ideas to emerge.
We discuss when suspending judgement is productive and when evaluation is needed.
We explore how premature judgement can shut down creative thinking and innovative solutions.
We practise evaluating ideas from perspectives different from our own before making a judgement.
We reflect on times when suspending judgement led us to better ideas or fairer conclusions.
We develop strategies for recognising when our initial judgement might be premature.
